reviews.review_text:
Bro the couches in the cabin are actually so terrible it genuinely ruins the entire vibe of being there. Like I’m not even exaggerating when I say they might be the most uncomfortable couches I’ve ever sat on in my life. The second you sit down, you already regret it because there is literally zero cushion. They’re so flat it feels like somebody took all the stuffing out years ago and just decided “yeah this is fine.” No it is not fine. At all. It literally feels like sitting on cardboard wrapped in fabric. A cabin is supposed to be cozy too, that’s the whole point. You’re supposed to walk in, throw yourself onto a huge soft couch, relax, maybe watch movies or sit by the fire or whatever. But these couches completely destroy that feeling because they feel like they were designed to make people uncomfortable on purpose. I swear even sitting there for twenty minutes makes my back start hurting. There’s no support anywhere. Your body just awkwardly sinks into nothing while somehow still feeling hard at the same time. I genuinely do not understand how furniture can fail this badly.

And don’t even get me started on how insanely small they are. Like who were these couches made for? Toddlers? There’s literally no room to sit normally. The second more than one person sits down it feels cramped and awkward. You can’t stretch your legs out, you can’t lay down properly, and if you try to get comfortable you end up elbowing somebody because there’s absolutely no space. It’s so annoying because every time I try to chill there I spend the whole time adjusting myself every five seconds trying to find a position that doesn’t suck. Spoiler alert: there isn’t one. The armrests are rock hard, the cushions are flatter than my motivation to do anything productive, and the backrests somehow hit at the worst angle possible. Whoever picked these couches genuinely deserves jail time because there’s no way anybody sat on them and thought “yeah these are comfortable.” Absolutely not. These things feel like props from a low-budget horror movie cabin where everything is meant to look old and miserable.

The craziest part is how they somehow make you more tired instead of helping you relax. Like after sitting on them for a while, your whole body starts feeling weird. Your lower back hurts, your neck hurts, your legs go numb, and you start getting irrationally angry over a piece of furniture. It’s honestly impressive how bad they are. You know couches are supposed to be one of the best parts of a cabin, right? Especially when it’s cold outside and everyone’s inside hanging out. But instead of making the place feel warm and cozy, these couches make the whole room feel depressing. Nobody even looks comfortable sitting there. Everyone’s always shifting around, leaning weirdly, or standing up after like ten minutes because they can’t deal with it anymore. At that point just put camping chairs in the living room because at least those are honest about being uncomfortable.

Honestly I would rather sit on the floor at this point. At least the floor doesn’t pretend to be comfortable and then disappoint me. The couches trick you every time because from a distance you think maybe they’ll be decent, and then the second you sit down you realize you’ve made a horrible mistake. It completely ruins movie nights too because instead of focusing on the movie, I’m focused on how badly my spine is suffering. Trying to sleep on those couches is even worse. That’s not sleeping, that’s surviving. Whoever says they can comfortably sleep on those things is either lying or has superhuman pain tolerance. There is genuinely nothing redeeming about them. They’re too flat, too small, too stiff, too old, and too uncomfortable to exist in a place that’s supposed to be relaxing. A cabin should feel peaceful and cozy, but those couches make me feel like I’m being punished for sitting down.
Stayed in the cottage, beautiful old building, lovely caravan park, even with teenagers on school surfing trip camping across it was very relaxing! Recommended!
We had a powered site on great lawn and the showers are some of the best.
Lachie and Charlie are very helpful. Camp Kitchen is great. Very relaxing place for base camp. Great location to the Howling Wolf which we had an awesome feed there and quite reasonable.

Great location, just a short drive from the beach. There is also a convenience store nearby with basic supplies, which is very handy for last minute purchases.

The caravan park itself is fairly simple, without many attractions for kids. That said, there is a playground onsite and a bike pump track right across the road, which offer a few good options. The games room with table tennis, darts, and foosball is also a nice addition. The camp kitchen is spacious and pleasant to use.

Staff were very friendly and the amenities were clean. Internet access is only available inside the camp kitchen. One downside is that the park only has bore water, however this is completely understandable given the region.

Overall, we had a pleasant stay and would happily return. Unfortunately, we cannot give five stars due to the limited facilities and the bore water, which place it behind more fully equipped parks. Still, it is an excellent option and well worth considering.
Top location and atmosphere. Well laid out with good facilities for families with kids. Highly recommended.

Great location just down the road from some beautiful scenery and food stops, the staff are extremely welcoming and help with any enquiries! The camps sites are well spaced close to amenities that are clean and the showers pressure/temperature worked exceptionally well. the camp kitchen is very spacious, burners work great and if you're interested they have darts and ping pong available that's extremely fun and a great way to escape the heat during the afternoon.
Great caravan park have friends that have on site van there years was great beaches stunning lovely people that work there would definitely go back 😀
A bit average with flies galore. Don’t forget the aerogard or even your fly net! Not really much there but still very nice friendly people.
Good spot, nice grassed drive through. Toilets and showers a little out dated but mostly clean and tidy. Camp kitchen great.
Another nice 'out-of-the-way' spot, that is quite popular yet peaceful at the same time. Lots to see!
Well , very good place but the kitchen is not . Plenty of birds poo and allowed dogs to pee on the floor . Never seen that before . Very disappointed

This unique park is one of our favourites. It’s small but cosy. The lawned powered sites are large. Amenities clean. Communal area quaint and the owners, well I can’t say enough on how warm, welcoming and informative they are. A great family run business. ||Don’t bother with the local pub

Called to change booking 6 days in advance but Charlie told me I’ve lost the whole amount of two nights stay as it wasn’t 21 days in advance. Which is a joke as we only booked 14 days in advance. And he was rude. Do not recommended at all. Never had this amount of rudeness at any other park in Australia. Thumbs down.

We were there in August and had the place to ourselves. It was fantastic. Green grass. Clean stones for our car with roof top tent. The camp kitchen was huge with a nice big tv. The internet seemed to work well. The bathrooms where clean and large. Lots of hot water. The guy who checked us in was very friendly, helpful and relaxed. Funny name, great place.
Great park, facilities were clean and well maintained.  Smallish park but highly recommended.
We were the only ones there so nice and quiet.good clean facilities great camp kitchen . Good rates.
Good
Awesome atmosphere fantastic people good trip away in a beautiful part of sa
Nice park with country feel

Nice spot to camp
Clean facilities

Great little park with very accomodating hosts. The sites are large and grassy. Camp kitchen facilities are awesome and the bathrooms pass the test. Great location if you’re looking to venture south down the YP.
Great rate cool owners
Only dropped into the kiosk, very friendly staff.

Stayed in one of the basic cabins. Simple, clean and very affordable. The caravan park is tree-lined and well maintained. Had to go outside for WiFi but wasn’t an issue. Great service and info folder. Walking distance to the pub. Thanks!

The best place to stay. We have been a few times, and it is always great. We stay in the cabins and it works really well for my family of 3, lots of room for more people. People in the kiosk are friendly, very helpful, and  knowledgeable in fishing spots.

Friendly and helpful service from all the team.
I really appreciated the park, particularly the beautifully maintained grassy caravan/motorhome parking. It's so nice to have grass under my feet after much  time in dust and sand.
Great place for a few nights  while touring the Yorke.

Very friendly & helpful Staff.
My Unit was really clean and well equipped. Close to amenities.
Good parking for my Campervan.
Park was well maintained and Camp Kitchen is fabulous!

The caravan was good but we were the only ones there. Booked for 3 nights but left after 2. Did a couple of day trips but the towns were like ghost towns. Had to really hunt around just to find a coffee. Pubs & shops were closed. Would leave your trip until much later into the summer. Looks like there would be much more activity then. The Dhilba Guuranda Innes National Park was great though and very much worth a visit. Great coastal scenery & lots of emus.
Very Clean and staff were Amazing very polite and helpful made you feel a part of the community nice fre pit evening highly recommend this place

Excellent smaller park. Some of the best grass sites we have seen in a long time.

Great amenities, even if they are older. Fantastic camp kitchen and playground for the kids.

The water for the park of ONLY bore water, no good for drinking, quite salty.

There are a lot of school groups, so sometimes the camp kitchen or toilets are jam packed, but overall they we very respectful of other campers.

We would definitely stay again

grassed areas amenities  clean.
Manager friendly and helpful.
Only fault some of the sites tight to get into.

Beautiful caravan park. We got a generous unpowered site. The grass is lovely, especially after camping on dirt all week haha. There is a bike pump track across the road which was great for our active toddler! As well as the playground. Nice kitchen. Bathrooms clean and well maintained. Highly recommend!

Very friendly, helpful owners. Very accommodating and open to sharing of local information. Nice big lawned sites. Amenities are OK, a bit tired and old but need to be  cleaned more regularly, especially when there are large groups like school kids etc. A very peaceful & relaxing caravan park.
They couldn't find our reservation when we arrived but worked with us and got us a nice spot on the grass. Great grassy sites make a difference.
Great place to stop and relax, great prices 👍

Great park! Grass is immaculate, even weeded by hand. Very friendly, informative and helpful staff. Relaxed vibe. Amazing camp kitchen. Good sized sites.
Originally planned on going to Burners Beach, drove in lots of people and rubbish overflowing, drove out. Glad we came here instead!!
Well maintained caravan park. Very nice manager, very friendly and helpful. Thankyou. Sharon and Bob
A quiet and clean little park close to the beach. The thickest grass I've ever seen in a park, just a joy to camp on!

A fantastic park, really well placed to explore this part of the Yorke.  Really well maintained, grass sites and an excellent camp kitchen.  Charlie was totally welcoming and had great tips for local sites and fishing.  Berry Bay was about an 8 minute drive and gave up some great fish.  We were told this is the only privately owned CVP on the Yorke, which is reflected in the standard and attention to detail.  Tavern is a 2 minute walk.  Wonderfully quiet and definitely worth a stop.
Cabins are tired but the grounds are great...good camping and van facilities...great overnighter

Looks great, green grass and well cared for,the only problem was there is another ISP so i haven't had that slow an internet connection since the early days of internet, there is WiFi but only in the kitchen area, being old, disabled, from the heat of the NT so unable take my monitor and sound system plus laptop over there, got through eventually, I would go there for the adventure though.

Booked to stay and had to change our plans. Booking website not very good. Couldn’t book online and their terms and conditions obscure. Had to email or call them to book. Took deposit but didn’t get a receipt so no idea what I paid. Then they only refund 21 days notice or more. Bit rough but maybe they need all the money they can get. Park is about 2klms from town.

Location is awesome, it's so quiet and pretty. The caravan space is a good size. The toilet and shower facilities were spotless. There's a great pub about a five minute walk away, with wonderful publicans and fabulous food.
